_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists VA as 'non-photo ID requested'. SoS confirms voters may show a non-photo document (utility bill, bank statement, paycheck, government document with name and address) or a photo ID. Virginia implemented expanded no-excuse absentee voting (any registered voter may vote absentee).

earlyVoting.notes: No-excuse in-person absentee (early voting) begins 45 days before the election and ends the Saturday before. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
No-excuse in-person absentee voting begins 45 days before Election Day and ends the Saturday before. Saturday early voting is required. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
No-excuse in-person absentee voting available beginning 45 days before Election Day through the Saturday before. Contact your local registrar for locations and hours.

acceptedIds: Virginia driver's license or ID card
Any U.S. or Virginia government-issued photo ID
Student ID from a Virginia college or university (with photo)
Utility bill, bank statement, government check, paycheck, or other government document showing name and address
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo IDs used for voting should not be expired. Non-photo documents showing name and address are also acceptable. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: If a voter cannot provide an acceptable form of ID, they may cast a provisional ballot and present acceptable ID to the general registrar by noon on the Friday after the election.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Virginia does not prohibit phones in polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or otherwise interfere with other voters.
additionalRules: Open primary: Virginia voters do not register by party. Any voter may participate in any party's primary.
No legislative primary runoffs in Virginia.

eligibility: Any registered Virginia voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the local registrar 5 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the registrar by 7 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 3 days after
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive by 7 p.m. Election Day, or be postmarked by Election Day and received within 3 days after.
